content:
Working with Polygot
polygot extract <file-path> [--out <json-path>]Extracts strings from TSX/JSX files and save in a json file in polygot supported format.
polygot scan <dir> [--ext .tsx,.jsx] [--out <path>]Same as extract but for directory. ext defines what extension of files to consider in directory.
polygot apply <file> [translations.json] [--out <path>] [--dry-run] [--no-backup]
[--adapter i18next|react-intl]This command applies Polygot support JSON file to a TSX/JSX and rebuild it. You can also use the --dry-run flag to preview the changes in your terminal without touching your files.

• Standard Merge: It takes a translations.json file and merges the translated strings back into the Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) of your code.

• The Framework Adapter: If you pass an adapter flag (like --adapter i18next or --adapter react-intl), Polygot will actually refactor your code for you. It replaces the hardcoded strings with framework call sites (e.g., t('id')), and it even auto-injects the necessary imports and hooks at the top of your file.
polygot audit <file> [--out <path>]Polygot is great but not perfect. Not everything can be cleanly auto-extracted. Dynamic runtime expressions (like {apiResponse.message}) often require manual handling. Running this command gives you a clear list of these tricky dynamic spots so you can manually review them without having to comb through the entire file yourself.
polygot watch <dir> [ - ext .tsx,.jsx] [ - out <path>]It works just like scan, but runs continuously in the background, updating automatically whenever you save a file. Perfect for local dev loops or CI/CD pipelines. Press Ctrl+C to stop.

Polygot Configuration
Polygot is designed to be highly configurable to match your project’s specific needs. You can control its behavior by creating a .polygotrc.json file in your project’s root directory.
{
"targetAttributes": ["placeholder", "title", "alt", "label", "aria-label", "tooltip"],
"ignoredDirs": ["node_modules", ".git", "dist", "build", "coverage"],
"extensions": [".tsx", ".jsx"],
"prettier": { "singleQuote": false }
}
• targetAttributes: Tells Polygot to not only look for text between HTML tags, but also to extract strings from specific attributes like alt text on images or aria-labels for accessibility.
Ready to automate your i18n pipeline. Get access to Polygot now.
https://github.com/vipulls-69/polygot
